title: Executive Order 12937—Declassification of Selected Records Within the National Archives of the United States

flags.justification: This declassifies records, reducing rather than creating new restrictive authority.
A clear, specific action with a 30-day implementation deadline.
Single purpose: declassifying the listed records.
This removes a classification restriction rather than creating a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A one-time declassification action grants no ongoing authority.
This expands public access, the opposite of removing oversight or transparency.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A specific, one-time transparency action with no broad authority claim.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.

Sec. 2's preservation of other, non-national-security FOIA exemptions maintains already-existing, distinct legal protections rather than creating a new carve-out from this order's own declassification action.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. A clean, transparency-expanding order.
